ma-proofbench-0005:
import Mathlib

open scoped ComplexConjugate

theorem ma_proofbench_l1_04 {U : Set ℂ} (hUopen : IsOpen U) (hUconn : IsConnected U)
    (f : ℂ → ℂ) (hf : DifferentiableOn ℂ f U)
    (hconj : DifferentiableOn ℂ (fun z => conj (f z)) U) :
    ∃ c : ℂ, Set.EqOn f (fun _ => c) U := by
  -- Both f and conj∘f are holomorphic on U
  -- Write f = u+iv. Cauchy-Riemann for f: u_x=v_y, u_y=-v_x
  -- Cauchy-Riemann for conj∘f = u-iv: u_x=-v_y, u_y=v_x
  -- Adding: 2u_x=0 ⇒ u_x=0; subtracting: 2v_y=0 ⇒ v_y=0
  -- All partial derivatives vanish → f is constant on connected U
  have h_deriv_zero : ∀ z ∈ U, deriv f z = 0 := by
    intro z hz
    have hfz := hf.differentiableAt (hUopen.mem_nhds hz)
    have hconjz := hconj.differentiableAt (hUopen.mem_nhds hz)
    -- If both f and conj∘f are ℂ-differentiable at z, then f'(z)=0
    -- (f holomorphic ⇒ ∂̄f=0; conj∘f holomorphic ⇒ ∂f=0)
    sorry
  -- Vanishing derivative on connected open set ⇒ constant
  rcases U.eq_empty_or_nonempty with hE | ⟨z₀, hz₀⟩
  · refine ⟨0, λ z hz => (hE hz).elim⟩
  · refine ⟨f z₀, λ z hz => ?_⟩
    have h := hUconn.isConstant_of_hasDerivAt (λ w hw => ?_) (hf.differentiableOn)
    sorry
ma-proofbench-0008:
import Mathlib

open Filter Topology

theorem ma_proofbench_l1_07 (a b : ℝ) (hab : a < b) (f : ℕ → (Set.Icc a b) → ℝ) (g : (Set.Icc a b) → ℝ)
    (hmono : ∀ n : ℕ, Monotone (f n))
    (hpt : ∀ x : Set.Icc a b, Tendsto (fun n => f n x) atTop (𝓝 (g x)))
    (hgcont : Continuous g) :
    Monotone g ∧ TendstoUniformly (fun n x => f n x) g atTop := by
  -- Dini's theorem variant: monotone (in n) sequence on compact set converging
  -- pointwise to continuous limit → uniform convergence
  have hgmono : Monotone g := by
    intro x y hxy
    apply le_of_tendsto (hpt x)
    refine eventually_of_forall (λ n => hmono n hxy)
  have h_compact : IsCompact (Set.Icc a b : Set ((Set.Icc a b) → ℝ)) := by
    -- The interval [a,b] is compact in ℝ
    sorry
  -- Apply Dini's theorem from mathlib: tendstoUniformly_of_tendsto_antitone
  -- or tendstoUniformly_of_tendsto_monotone on compact set
  have h_uniform : TendstoUniformly (fun n x => f n x) g atTop := by
    -- Need to verify monotonicity in n: f_n(x) ≤ f_{n+1}(x) or reverse
    -- hmono gives monotonicity in x for each fixed n, not in n
    -- This is stated as a variant: monotone functions on compact interval,
    -- pointwise convergence to continuous limit → uniform convergence
    sorry
  exact And.intro hgmono h_uniform
ma-proofbench-0010:
import Mathlib

open Complex Topology

noncomputable def f_lacunary (z : ℂ) : ℂ := ∑' j : ℕ, ((2 : ℂ)⁻¹ ^ j) * z ^ ((2 : ℕ) ^ j)

theorem f_lacunary_holomorphic : DifferentiableOn ℂ f_lacunary (Metric.ball (0 : ℂ) 1) := by
  -- Power series Σ a_j z^{2^j} with a_j = 2^{-j}
  -- For |z| ≤ r < 1: |z^{2^j}| ≤ r^{2^j} ≤ r^{2j} for j≥2
  -- Terms bounded by (r²/2)^j → geometric convergence
  -- Weierstrass M-test gives uniform convergence on compact subsets → holomorphic
  sorry

theorem f_lacunary_continuousOn_closedBall : ContinuousOn f_lacunary (Metric.closedBall (0 : ℂ) 1) := by
  -- For |z| ≤ 1: |z^{2^j}| ≤ 1, terms bounded by 2^{-j}
  -- Σ 2^{-j} converges → Weierstrass M-test → uniform convergence on closed disk
  -- Each partial sum is continuous, uniform limit is continuous
  sorry
ma-proofbench-0012:
import Mathlib

open Set

theorem ma_proofbench_l1_11 (a : ℝ) (ha : 0 < a) (f : ℝ → ℝ)
    (hf_C2 : ContDiffOn ℝ 2 f (Icc (-a) a))
    (hf_even : ∀ x : ℝ, x ∈ Icc (-a) a → f x = f (-x)) :
    ∃ g : ℝ → ℝ, ContDiffOn ℝ 1 g (Icc 0 (a ^ (2 : ℕ))) ∧
      ∀ x : ℝ, x ∈ Icc (-a) a → f x = g (x ^ (2 : ℕ)) := by
  -- Define g(t) = f(√t) for t ∈ [0,a²]
  -- Since f is even, f'(0)=0, so g'(t) = f'(√t)/(2√t) has limit f''(0)/2 as t→0⁺
  -- This ensures g is C¹
  set g := λ t : ℝ => if h : 0 ≤ t then f (Real.sqrt t) else f 0 with hg_def
  refine ⟨g, ?_, λ x hx => ?_⟩
  · -- g is C¹ on [0,a²]
    refine (hf_C2.comp (λ t => Real.sqrt t) ?_ ?_).of_le (by norm_num : (1 : ℕ∞) ≤ 2)
    · -- √t is C² on (0,a²]
      refine contDiffOn_sqrt.mono (Set.Icc_subset_Ioi_right ?_)
      sorry
    sorry
  · -- f(x) = g(x²)
    rw [hg_def]
    have hx_sq_nonneg : 0 ≤ x ^ 2 := pow_two_nonneg x
    simp [hx_sq_nonneg, Real.sqrt_sq_eq_abs, abs_of_le (hx.2.trans ha.le)]
    -- Use evenness: f(x) = f(|x|)
    sorry
ma-proofbench-0015:
import Mathlib

open Complex

theorem ma_proofbench_l1_14 : ∃ a : ℕ → ℂ,
    (∀ z : ℂ, Summable (fun k : ℕ => a k * z ^ k)) ∧
    Set.Infinite {z : ℂ | (∑' k : ℕ, a k * z ^ k) = 0} ∧
    (∃ k : ℕ, a k ≠ 0) := by
  -- Example: f(z) = sin(πz)/(πz) = Π_{n=1}^∞ (1 - z²/n²)
  -- Entire function, vanishes at all nonzero integers, not identically zero
  -- Power series: Σ (-1)^k π^{2k} z^{2k} / (2k+1)!
  set a := λ k : ℕ =>
    if h : Even k then ((-1 : ℂ) ^ (k / 2)) * ((π : ℂ) ^ k) / ((Nat.factorial (k + 1) : ℂ))
    else 0 with ha
  refine ⟨a, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
  · intro z
    refine Summable.of_norm_bounded ?_ ?_
    sorry
  · -- Zeros at all nonzero integers (infinite set)
    have h_zeros : ∀ n : ℤ, n ≠ 0 → (∑' k : ℕ, a k * ((n : ℂ) ^ k)) = 0 := by
      intro n hn
      -- f(n) = sin(πn)/(πn) = 0 since sin(πn)=0 for integer n
      sorry
    refine Set.Infinite.mono (λ z hz => ?_) (Set.infinite_univ)
    sorry
  · refine ⟨0, ?_⟩; simp [ha]
ma-proofbench-0016:
import Mathlib

open MeasureTheory Filter

theorem ma_proofbench_l1_15 (d : ℕ) (f g : (Fin d → ℝ) → ℝ)
    (hf : MeasureTheory.Integrable f) (hg_meas : Measurable g)
    (hg_bdd : Bornology.IsBounded (Set.range g)) :
    UniformContinuous (fun x => ∫ y, (f (x - y)) * (g y)) := by
  -- (f*g)(x) = ∫ f(x-y)g(y)dy
  -- |(f*g)(x+h) - (f*g)(x)| ≤ ‖g‖_∞ · ‖τ_{-h}f - f‖_{L¹}
  -- Translation is continuous in L¹: lim_{h→0} ‖τ_h f - f‖_{L¹} = 0
  -- Hence f*g is uniformly continuous
  rcases hg_bdd with ⟨M, hM⟩
  have hg_bound : ∀ y, |g y| ≤ M := by
    intro y; have := hM (Set.mem_range_self y); exact this
  refine Metric.uniformContinuous_iff.mpr ?_
  intro ε hε
  -- Need δ > 0 such that ‖h‖ < δ ⇒ |(f*g)(x+h)-(f*g)(x)| < ε
  -- Use L¹ continuity of translation
  sorry
ma-proofbench-0017:
import Mathlib

theorem ma_proofbench_l1_16 {a b : ℝ} (hab : a < b) {f : ℝ → ℝ}
    (hf : ContDiffOn ℝ 1 f (Set.Icc a b)) (hfa : f a = 0) (hfb : f b = 0)
    (hnorm : (∫ x in a..b, (f x) ^ 2) = 1) :
    (∫ x in a..b, x * f x * deriv f x) = (-(1 / 2 : ℝ)) := by
  -- Integration by parts: d/dx[x·f²/2] = f²/2 + x·f·f'
  -- ∫_a^b (f²/2 + xff') = [x·f²/2]_a^b = 0 (since f(a)=f(b)=0)
  -- So ∫_a^b f²/2 + ∫_a^b xff' = 0
  -- 1/2 + ∫_a^b xff' = 0 ⇒ ∫_a^b xff' = -1/2
  have h_boundary : b * (f b)^2 / 2 - a * (f a)^2 / 2 = 0 := by rw [hfa, hfb]; norm_num
  have h_deriv_antideriv : ∀ x ∈ Set.Icc a b,
    deriv (λ t => t * (f t)^2 / 2) x = (f x)^2 / 2 + x * f x * deriv f x := by
    intro x hx
    have hx' : HasDerivAt (λ t => t * (f t)^2 / 2) ((f x)^2 / 2 + x * f x * deriv f x) x := by
      -- product rule + chain rule
      sorry
    exact deriv_eq hx'
  have hFTC : (∫ x in a..b, ((f x)^2 / 2 + x * f x * deriv f x)) =
      b * (f b)^2 / 2 - a * (f a)^2 / 2 := by
    -- Fundamental theorem of calculus
    sorry
  rw [h_boundary] at hFTC
  have h_split : (∫ x in a..b, ((f x)^2 / 2 + x * f x * deriv f x)) =
      (∫ x in a..b, (f x)^2 / 2) + (∫ x in a..b, x * f x * deriv f x) := integral_add ?_ ?_
  rw [h_split] at hFTC
  have h_first_integral : (∫ x in a..b, (f x)^2 / 2) = 1/2 := by
    rw [integral_div, integral_const_mul, hnorm]; norm_num
  rw [h_first_integral] at hFTC
  linarith
ma-proofbench-0021:
import Mathlib

theorem ma_proofbench_l1_20 (F : ℂ → ℂ)
    (hHolo : DifferentiableOn ℂ F {z : ℂ | 0 < z.im})
    (hBound : ∀ z, 0 < z.im → ‖F z‖ ≤ 1)
    (hFi : F Complex.I = 0) :
    ∀ z : ℂ, 0 < z.im → ‖F z‖ ≤ ‖(z - Complex.I) / (z + Complex.I)‖ := by
  -- This is the Schwarz-Pick lemma for the upper half-plane
  -- The Cayley transform φ(z) = (z-i)/(z+i) maps ℍ → D, with φ(i) = 0
  -- Define G(w) = F(φ⁻¹(w)) for w ∈ D. Then G: D → D, G(0) = 0.
  -- By Schwarz lemma, |G(w)| ≤ |w| for all w ∈ D.
  -- Hence |F(z)| = |G(φ(z))| ≤ |φ(z)| = |(z-i)/(z+i)|.
  intro z hz
  -- The Cayley transform and its inverse
  -- Formal verification requires composing with the conformal map
  sorry
ma-proofbench-0023:
import Mathlib

theorem ma_proofbench_l1_22 {E : Type u} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace ℝ E] [CompleteSpace E]
    (T : E →ₗ[ℝ] StrongDual ℝ E) (hsym : ∀ x y : E, (T x) y = (T y) x) :
    ∃ C : ℝ, 0 ≤ C ∧ ∀ x : E, ‖T x‖ ≤ C * ‖x‖ := by
  -- Hellinger-Toeplitz theorem: symmetric linear operator defined everywhere
  -- on a Banach space is automatically bounded
  -- Proof via Closed Graph Theorem / Uniform Boundedness Principle
  -- For each y, the linear functional x ↦ ⟨Tx, y⟩ = ⟨Ty, x⟩ is continuous
  -- By the Uniform Boundedness Principle, sup_{‖x‖≤1} ‖Tx‖ < ∞
  -- Hence T is bounded
  sorry
ma-proofbench-0025:
import Mathlib

theorem ma_proofbench_l1_24 (a b : ℕ → ℝ)
    (ha : ∀ x : ℝ, |x| < 1 → Summable (fun k : ℕ => a k * x ^ k))
    (hb : ∀ x : ℝ, |x| < 1 → Summable (fun k : ℕ => b k * x ^ k))
    (hEq : ∀ n : ℕ, (∑' k : ℕ, a k * ((1 : ℝ) / (n + 2)) ^ k) =
      (∑' k : ℕ, b k * ((1 : ℝ) / (n + 2)) ^ k)) :
    ∀ k : ℕ, a k = b k := by
  -- Identity theorem for analytic functions
  -- f(x) = Σ a_k x^k and g(x) = Σ b_k x^k are analytic on (-1,1)
  -- They agree on the set {1/2, 1/3, 1/4, ...} which has limit point 0
  -- By the identity theorem: f ≡ g on (-1,1) ⇒ a_k = b_k for all k
  intro k
  set f := λ x : ℝ => ∑' k : ℕ, a k * x ^ k with hf
  set g := λ x : ℝ => ∑' k : ℕ, b k * x ^ k with hg
  -- f and g are analytic on (-1,1) and agree on a set with limit point 0
  -- Hence all Taylor coefficients match
  sorry
ma-proofbench-0026:
import Mathlib

theorem ma_proofbench_l1_25 {f : ℂ → ℂ} {U : Set ℂ}
    (hU : IsOpen U)
    (hclosed : Metric.closedBall (0 : ℂ) 1 ⊆ U)
    (hf : DifferentiableOn ℂ f U)
    (hnonconst : ∃ z₁ ∈ U, ∃ z₂ ∈ U, f z₁ ≠ f z₂)
    (hboundary : ∀ z, z ∈ Metric.sphere (0 : ℂ) 1 → ‖f z‖ = 1) :
    Metric.ball (0 : ℂ) 1 ⊆ f '' U := by
  -- By Maximum Modulus Principle: max_{D̄} |f| occurs on ∂D
  -- Since |f| = 1 on ∂D and f is non-constant, |f(z)| < 1 for |z| < 1
  -- Also f(∂D) ⊆ ∂D (since |f|=1 on boundary)
  -- By the Open Mapping Theorem, f(D) is open
  -- Combined with boundary behavior, f(D) must contain the whole disk D
  intro w hw
  have hw_norm : ‖w‖ < 1 := Metric.mem_ball.mp hw
  -- Need to show w ∈ f(U)
  -- Use Rouché / argument principle: winding number of f(e^{iθ}) around w is 1
  sorry
ma-proofbench-0027:
import Mathlib

theorem ma_proofbench_l1_26 (f : ℂ → ℂ) (P : ℂ) (r : ℝ) (hr : 0 < r)
    (hhol : ∃ U : Set ℂ, IsOpen U ∧ Metric.closedBall P r ⊆ U ∧ DifferentiableOn ℂ f U)
    (hnot : ¬ ∀ z ∈ Metric.ball P r, f z = 0) :
    (Set.Finite {z : ℂ | z ∈ Metric.ball P r ∧ f z = 0}) := by
  -- Non-zero holomorphic function has isolated zeros
  -- In the compact set D̄(P,r), an infinite set of zeros would have a limit point
  -- By the identity theorem, f ≡ 0 on the connected component, contradicting hnot
  -- Therefore the zero set in D(P,r) is finite
  rcases hhol with ⟨U, hU_open, hU_closedBall, hf⟩
  by_contra h_infinite
  have h_infinite' : Set.Infinite {z : ℂ | z ∈ Metric.ball P r ∧ f z = 0} := h_infinite
  -- Infinite set in compact metric ball has a limit point in the closed ball
  obtain ⟨z₀, hz₀⟩ := Set.infinite.exists_limitPoint_of_infinite h_infinite'
    (Metric.isCompact_closedBall P r)
  -- z₀ is a limit point of zeros; f(z₀) = 0 by continuity
  -- By the identity theorem (on the connected ball), f ≡ 0, contradiction
  sorry
ma-proofbench-0028:
import Mathlib

def realAxis : Set ℂ := {z | z.im = 0}

theorem ma_proofbench_l1_27 {f : ℂ → ℂ} (hf_cont : Continuous f)
    (hf_holo_off_axes : DifferentiableOn ℂ f ({z : ℂ | z.re ≠ 0 ∧ z.im ≠ 0}ᶜ)ᶜ) :
    Differentiable ℂ f := by
  -- f is continuous on ℂ and holomorphic off the coordinate axes
  -- By Morera's theorem: for any triangle T, ∫_{∂T} f = 0
  -- Subdivide triangles that cross axes into smaller ones
  -- Triangles not crossing axes: Goursat gives zero integral
  -- Triangles crossing axes: approximate by non-crossing → integral → 0
  -- Hence f is holomorphic everywhere
  sorry
ma-proofbench-0030:
import Mathlib

theorem ma_proofbench_l1_29 (f : ℝ → ℝ)
    (hf : f = fun x : ℝ => ∫ t in x..(x + 1), Real.sin (t ^ 2))
    (x : ℝ) (hx : 0 < x) :
    |f x| < 1 / x := by
  -- f(x) = ∫_x^{x+1} sin(t²) dt
  -- Integration by parts: u = 1/(2t), dv = 2t sin(t²) dt = -d(cos(t²))
  -- ∫ sin(t²) dt = -cos(t²)/(2t) - ∫ cos(t²)/(2t²) dt
  -- Evaluate from x to x+1:
  -- |f(x)| ≤ 1/(2x) + 1/(2(x+1)) + ∫_x^{x+1} 1/(2t²) dt = 1/x
  -- Strict inequality follows since cos(t²) oscillates
  rw [hf]
  sorry
